title: Ocugen Q1 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
text:
Ocugen Q1 2021 Earnings Call Summary

Key Business Developments

COVAXIN Vaccine Program:
Ocugen is advancing its partnership with Bharat Biotech to bring COVAXIN to the U.S. market. The company submitted a comprehensive drug master file to the FDA and is preparing an Emergency Use Authorization application. Management noted that the current crisis in India is hampering some of the data collection efforts by our co-development partner, Bharat Biotech.

The vaccine demonstrated 78% overall efficacy and 100% effectiveness against severe COVID-19 in Phase III interim results. It showed potential effectiveness against multiple variants including the B.1.617 (Indian) variant.

Ophthalmology Pipeline:
OCU400 (gene therapy for retinitis pigmentosa): Manufacturing scaled to 200-liter capacity; toxicology studies in progress; IND filing expected later in 2021
OCU200 (biologic for macular edema): Phase I/II trial planned for 2022
OCU410 (dry age-related macular degeneration): Phase I/II trial planned for 2022

Financial Highlights
Q1 2021 cash position: $44.9 million (vs. $24.2 million at year-end 2020)
April 2021: $100 million raised through registered direct offering
Q1 R&D expenses: $2.9 million (up from $1.7 million year-over-year)
Net loss: $7.1 million ($0.04 per share)

Management Outlook
Ocugen expects to file the COVAXIN EUA application within weeks and anticipates FDA review within 3-4 weeks post-filing. The company is also pursuing BARDA purchase commitments and planning pediatric studies for COVAXIN.
